title: portulaca oleracea subsp. sativa
intro: Portulaca oleracea subsp. sativa, also known as the garden purslane or common purslane, is a type of succulent plant belonging to the purslane family (Portulacaceae). This plant is rich in medicinal properties, containing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, such as vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial, antiproliferative, and anti-hyperlipidemic activities. The health benefits of Portulaca oleracea subsp. sativa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anticancer properties, as well as its ability to act as a natural diuretic and aid in weight loss. Herbal preparations of Portulaca oleracea subsp. sativa include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
